question_text: When a compass is placed near the north pole of a bar magnet, which direction does the compass needle point?

options.label: A
options.text: Towards the north pole of the magnet

options.label: B
options.text: Towards the south pole of the magnet

options.label: D
options.text: It does not point in any particular direction

explanation: Compass needles always align with the direction of magnetic field lines. Since field lines point from north to south, the compass needle points towards the south pole of the magnet. The south pole of the magnet attracts the north pole of the compass needle.
